   <item>
      <title>Nuggets Will Consider All Options This Offseason Except Trading Nikola Jokic</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285528/Nuggets-Will-Consider-All-Options-This-Offseason-Except-Trading-Nikola-Jokic</link>
      <description><p>Denver Nuggets president Josh Kroenke declared Friday that "everything is on the table" this offseason following the team's first-round playoff exit, with the clear exception that they will not trade Nikole Jokic.</p>
<p>"I don't want to be masked in my frustration for how the season ended," Kroenke said at a 45-minute news conference at Ball Arena. "I think everything is gonna be on the table, outside of trading Nikola."</p>
<p>Kroenke did not rule out paying the NBA's costly repeater tax, which applies when a team finishes in luxury tax territory after doing so in three of the previous four seasons. The Nuggets avoided the tax this season after trading Hunter Tyson to the Brooklyn Nets before the February deadline. Two consecutive years under the threshold would reset their repeater clock through 2029-30.</p>
<p>Several league sources told The Denver Post they expect Denver to trade at least one starter this offseason, particularly if the team retains restricted free agent Peyton Watson. Pending contract extensions for Aaron Gordon, Christian Braun and Watson are projected to push the Nuggets into second-apron territory, which carries stricter roster-building restrictions.</p>
<p>Kroenke identified Jokic, Jamal Murray and Gordon as the team's core, noting that Michael Porter Jr. was previously considered part of that group before being traded for Cam Johnson last summer.</p>
<p>"When I say running it back, you're talking about a lot of different variations," Kroenke said. "Is it gonna be the exact same team? I don't think there's ever the exact same team. But are you talking about the same core group of players? Potentially."</p>
<p>If neither Murray nor Gordon is moved, sources indicate at least one of Braun, Johnson or Watson is likely to be dealt for salary relief.</p>
<p>Jokic is expected to sign a four-tear, $278 million contract extension with the Nuggets this offseason.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 08 May 2026 22:26:27 EDT</pubDate>

   <item>
      <title>Mike Schmitz Hired As General Manager Of Mavericks</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285527/Mike-Schmitz-Hired-As-General-Manager-Of-Mavericks</link>
      <description><p>The Dallas Mavericks have named Mike Schmitz as General Manager, the team announced Friday. Schmitz will report to President Masai Ujiri and oversee day-to-day basketball operations and strategic planning.</p>
<p>Schmitz joins Dallas after serving as Assistant General Manager with the Portland Trail Blazers, where he contributed to player evaluation, scouting, roster strategy and organizational planning. He is widely regarded as one of the NBA's top talent evaluators.</p>
<p>"Mike is one of the most respected evaluators and basketball minds in the NBA," Ujiri said. "He brings intelligence, discipline, humility and a relentless work ethic to everything he does. Just as importantly, he understands how to build an aligned, collaborative culture across every part of a basketball organization. We are building something special in Dallas, and Mike will be a major part of that vision."</p>
<p>"Joining the Dallas Mavericks is an incredible opportunity," Schmitz said. "I have tremendous respect for Masai, this ownership group and the vision they have for the future of this franchise. I'm excited to get to work alongside the talented people already in place and help build a championship-caliber organization."</p>
<p>Before joining Portland in May 2022, Schmitz spent five years as a draft analyst for ESPN. He has served as an assistant coach for the Ugandan national team since 2018 and worked as a global scout for DraftExpress since 2012. He is a 2012 graduate of the University of Arizona.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 08 May 2026 15:34:34 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285527/Mike-Schmitz-Hired-As-General-Manager-Of-Mavericks</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_dallasmavericks.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Darryn Peterson Says Creatine Usage Led To Cramping Issues</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285520/Darryn-Peterson-Says-Creatine-Usage-Led-To-Cramping-Issues</link>
      <description><p>Throughout his freshman season at Kansas, Darryn Peterson struggled with cramping issues that regularly knocked him out of games. Peterson's doctors believe it was his high usage of creatine that was causing the cramping.</p>
<p>"I'd never taken it before [he got to college]," Peterson said. "But after the season I took two weeks off and they did tests which showed my baseline level was already high. So, they said when I dosed [a process of increasing a dose over time to create maximum benefit at the beginning of taking a supplement], it must've made the levels unsafe."</p>
<p>Creatine is a supplement used to help increase muscle strength. Many young athletes use creatine to help them build up and maintain muscle growth.</p>
<p>In his lone season with the Jayhawks, Peterson missed 11 games, and was forced to leave several others early. Still, the 6-foot-6 guard showed enough talent that he's ranked second overall on ESPN's NBA Draft Top 100 prospects list.</p>
<p>With the cramping issues hopefully behind him, Peterson is working on his shooting and his on-ball playmaking ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft.</p>
<p>"I was off [the ball] most of the year, but some of that was me not really being myself," Peterson said. "So, coach was trying to figure out ways to help me still be effective without exerting too much. As the point guard you got to bring it up, you got to do everything.</p>
<p>"I've been thinking about how differently things could have been [at Kansas] if I didn't get hurt or have all this stuff going on. When I was out there, I felt like I still did all right. But there was another level of me that people didn't get to see."</p>
<p>Peterson is represented by <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/info/agent_clients/Darren-Matsubara/376">Darren Matsubara</a>. </p></description>

      <title>NBA Playoff Odds: All Four Second-Round Favorites Above 80 Percent To Advance After First Two Games</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285521/NBA-Playoff-Odds-All-Four-Second-Round-Favorites-Above-80-Percent-To-Advance-After-First-Two-Games</link>
      <description><p>Three of the four second-round NBA playoff series stand at 2-0, while the San Antonio Spurs and Minnesota Timberwolves are tied at 1-1 heading into Game 3 on Friday. The Oklahoma City Thunder, New York Knicks and Detroit Pistons each hold commanding series leads through the first two games, which could result in a Conference Semifinals short on drama if the trend continues.</p>
<p>The Thunder entered their series against Los Angeles Lakers as heavy favorites and have only extended that margin. With the Lakers missing Luka Doncic, Oklahoma City now sits at -10000 to close out the series, reflecting a 99 percent implied probability. That dominance has come despite Shai Gilgeous-Alexander operating below his typical MVP level, with role players Ajay Mitchell and Jared McCain highlighting the roster depth assembled by general manager Sam Presti.</p>
<p>The Knicks defeated the Philadelphia 76ers in Game 1 by a wide margin before winning more narrowly in Game 2, with Philadelphia missing Joel Embiid. New York is now -1000 and a 90 percent probability to reach the Eastern Conference Finals for the second straight season.</p>
<p>The Pistons took both home games against the Cleveland Cavaliers and sit at -400 to advance, an 80 percent implied probability.</p>
<p>In the only competitive series, the Spurs hold -450 favorite status against the Wolves despite the 1-1 split. Game 3 shifts to Minnesota in a pivotal road test for San Antonio.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/nba-championship-betting-odds/">Oklahoma City leads NBA championship futures at -175</a>. The Spurs follow at +350, with the Knicks at +850 and the Detroit Pistons at +1500.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Thunder Depth Dominates Lakers In Game 2 Amid Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Foul Trouble</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285519/Thunder-Depth-Dominates-Lakers-In-Game-2-Amid-Shai-Gilgeous-Alexander-Foul-Trouble</link>
      <description><p>The Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the Los Angeles Lakers 125-107 in Game 2 on Thursday, extending the Thunder's unbeaten postseason record and taking a 2-0 series lead. A 22-4 run sparked by reserve contributors proved decisive while reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander sat with foul trouble.</p>
<p>The Thunder surge began when Gilgeous-Alexander picked up his fourth foul just 86 seconds into the third quarter, with the Lakers holding a five-point lead. By the time he returned for the fourth quarter, Oklahoma City led by 13.</p>
<p>Gilgeous-Alexander, who tied Chet Holmgren for team-high scoring honors with 22 points, praised his teammates' execution during his absence.</p>
<p>"It was amazing," Gilgeous-Alexander said. "They strung together stops, and they played the right way offensively. I've got full confidence in those guys. Those guys know how to win basketball games. They've proven that. We've proven that. No matter who's on the floor, we can get the job done."</p>
<p>Ajay Mitchell, starting in place of injured All-NBA wing Jalen Williams, delivered 20 points and five assists. Seven of those points and three assists came during the critical third quarter. Mitchell was not part of the playoff rotation in his rookie season last year.</p>
<p>Jared McCain, acquired from the Philadelphia 76ers at the trade deadline, provided an immediate spark off the bench. He scored 18 points on 7-of-11 shooting, including 4-of-5 from three-point range, in just 18 minutes.</p>
<p>"Every time I see him shoot, I think it's going in," Holmgren said of McCain. "He's got just a certain type of confidence in him to come in the game cold after a lot of time sitting long stretches and then just bomb and make shots."</p>
<p>Holmgren contributed nine rebounds, three assists, four steals and two blocks. Oklahoma City outscored Los Angeles by 19 points across his 33 minutes. When the Lakers closed within five in the fourth quarter, Holmgren answered with a three-pointer and two free throws to restore a double-digit cushion.</p>
<p>"We've got guys that are competitors," coach Mark Daigneault said. "They see the challenges of the game as opportunities, and they attack them."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">The Thunder are 8.5 point favorites for Game 3 on Saturday at the Lakers</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 08 May 2026 07:22:02 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285519/Thunder-Depth-Dominates-Lakers-In-Game-2-Amid-Shai-Gilgeous-Alexander-Foul-Trouble</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Mitchell_Ajay_okc_250103.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Nuggets Retain David Adelman For Second Season With Full Front Office Support</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285526/Nuggets-Retain-David-Adelman-For-Second-Season-With-Full-Front-Office-Support</link>
      <description><p>Denver Nuggets president Josh Kroenke publicly backed head coach David Adelman on Friday despite the team's first-round playoff elimination by the Minnesota Timberwolves, confirming Adelman will return for a second season.</p>
<p>"I have full faith in Coach Adelman," Kroenke said at a news conference at Ball Arena. "I think he coached a hell of a season, all things considered."</p>
<p>Adelman, who turns 45 next week, guided Denver to a 54-28 record and the No. 3 seed in the Western Conference in his first full season as head coach. The Nuggets lost in six games to Minnesota, who finished the series without star guard Anthony Edwards and starting two-guard Donte DiVincenzo.</p>
<p>The first-round exit marked Denver's earliest postseason departure since 2022. Kroenke noted the organization does not make coaching changes lightly, referencing the 2025 dismissal of Michael Malone.</p>
<p>Adelman's debut season was defined by roster instability. He used 28 different starting lineups and navigated a month-long absence from three-time MVP Nikola Jokic, going 10-6 without the superstar center. The Nuggets closed the regular season on a 12-game winning streak.</p>
<p>"I thought that if this group was healthy, that this could be a 60-, 65-win team," Kroenke said.</p>
<p>Both Jokic and Jamal Murray defended Adelman following the season-ending loss, asserting the playoff result was not a reflection of his coaching.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 08 May 2026 15:24:16 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285526/Nuggets-Retain-David-Adelman-For-Second-Season-With-Full-Front-Office-Support</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Adelman_David_den_250410.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>How To Watch The WNBA</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285515/How-To-Watch-The-WNBA</link>
      <description><p>The wait is finally over. Following a record-breaking 2025, the WNBA is stepping into its landmark <strong data-path-to-node="0" data-index-in-node="99">30th anniversary season</strong> with the momentum of a runaway freight train. Between a lucrative new media rights deal and the arrival of two fresh expansion teams, the 2026 campaign isn't just a season-it's a global takeover.</p>
<p><a href="https://www.fubo.tv/stream/nba/?irad=1846663&irmp=3589123&subId1=RealGM" target="_blank" rel="noopener"> <input style="background-color: #bb681b; color: #ffffff; border-color: #d5d5d5; border-radius: 5px; cursor: pointer; border-width: 1px; padding: 10px; margin: 10px;" type="button" value="Watch WNBA on Fubo" /> </a></p>
<h3 data-path-to-node="4"><strong data-path-to-node="4" data-index-in-node="0">A New Landscape: Stars & Stuntin'</strong></h3>
<p data-path-to-node="5">It's been two years since <strong data-path-to-node="5" data-index-in-node="26">Caitlin Clark</strong> and <strong data-path-to-node="5" data-index-in-node="44">Angel Reese</strong> changed the gravity of the league, and the shockwaves are still felt. This April, the league was turned on its head when Reese was sent to the <strong data-path-to-node="5" data-index-in-node="199">Atlanta Dream</strong> in a blockbuster trade, instantly transforming the "A" into a title threat. Meanwhile, in Indiana, Caitlin Clark remains the league's most lethal offensive weapon for the Fever.</p>
<p data-path-to-node="6">The <strong data-path-to-node="6" data-index-in-node="4">Las Vegas Aces</strong> enter 2026 with a massive target on their jerseys. After snagging three of the last four championships, <strong data-path-to-node="6" data-index-in-node="123">A'ja Wilson</strong> is eyeing a "three-peat" (interrupted only by the Liberty's 2024 run). However, the road is more crowded than ever. With 15 teams now in the mix thanks to the <strong data-path-to-node="6" data-index-in-node="294">Toronto Tempo</strong> and <strong data-path-to-node="6" data-index-in-node="312">Portland Fire</strong>, the schedule is a beautiful brand of chaos.</p>
<h3 data-path-to-node="7"><strong data-path-to-node="7" data-index-in-node="0">When does the 2026 WNBA season start?</strong></h3>
<p data-path-to-node="8">The 2026 regular season officially launches <strong data-path-to-node="8" data-index-in-node="44">tomorrow, Friday, May 8, 2026</strong>.</p>
<p><a href="https://www.fubo.tv/stream/nba/?irad=1846663&irmp=3589123&subId1=RealGM" target="_blank" rel="noopener"> <input style="background-color: #bb681b; color: #ffffff; border-color: #d5d5d5; border-radius: 5px; cursor: pointer; border-width: 1px; padding: 10px; margin: 10px;" type="button" value="Watch WNBA on Fubo" /> </a></p>
<p data-path-to-node="9">Opening night is a certified gauntlet:</p>
<ul data-path-to-node="10">
<li>
<p data-path-to-node="10,0,0"><strong data-path-to-node="10,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">Historic Debut:</strong> The <strong data-path-to-node="10,0,0" data-index-in-node="20">Toronto Tempo</strong> play their first-ever game at home against the Washington Mystics.</p>
</li>

<p data-path-to-node="10,1,0"><strong data-path-to-node="10,1,0" data-index-in-node="0">Revenge SZN:</strong> <strong data-path-to-node="10,1,0" data-index-in-node="13">Angel Reese</strong> makes her Dream debut against her former team, the Chicago Sky.</p>
</li>
</ul>

<p data-path-to-node="12,0,0"><strong data-path-to-node="12,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">WNBA Tip-Off Weekend:</strong> May 8-10, 2026</p>

      <description><p>Toronto Raptors forward Brandon Ingram underwent surgery Friday to remove a heel spur from his right foot, the team announced. Dr. Martin O'Malley performed the procedure at Hospital for Special Surgery in New York.</p>
<p>Ingram is expected to make a full recovery and be available for training camp in September.</p>
<p>The 27-year-old was named an All-Star for the second time last season, leading the Raptors with 21.5 points per game while adding 5.6 rebounds and 3.7 assists across 77 starts.</p>
<p>Ingram re-signed with the Raptors last offseason on a three-year, $120 million deal.</p></description>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285518/PJ-Tucker-Announces-Retirement-From-NBA</link>
      <description><p>PJ Tucker announced his retirement from the NBA in <a href="https://www.instagram.com/pjtucker/p/DYDrHb3Fp82/">an Instagram post</a> on Thursday.</p>
<p>"20 years being my job but 40 plus years of not being able to fathom doing anything other than it. So here's to retiring from the NBA... because I will NEVER stop ballin," wrote Tucker.</p>
<p>Tucker played 19 seasons in the league with the Knicks, Raptors, Suns, Rockets, Bucks, Heat, 76ers and Clippers. </p>
<p>Tucker won an NBA title in 2021 with the Bucks.</p>
<p>Originally selected with the 35th pick by the Raptors in 2006, Tucker averaged 6.6 points and 5.4 rebounds for his career.</p></description>

      <title>Donovan Mitchell Says James Harden Will 'Figure It Out' After Scoring 10 Points In Game 2 Loss</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285517/Donovan-Mitchell-Says-James-Harden-Will-Figure-It-Out-After-Scoring-10-Points-In-Game-2-Loss</link>
      <description><p>Donovan Mitchell said James Harden would "figure it out" following the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285516/JB-Bickerstaff-Says-Pistons-Just-Wear-On-You-Following-Game-2-Win-Over-Cavs">Cleveland Cavaliers Game 2 loss to the Detroit Pistons</a>.</p>
<p>Harden finished with 10 points in the game while missing 10-of-13 shots.</p>
<p>It's the second straight game where Harden finished with more turnovers than field goals made.</p>
<p>"He's James Harden; we're not sitting here worried," Mitchell said following Thursday's loss. "He's going to figure this out."</p>
<p>The Cavs were outscored by 15 points with Harden on the floor.</p>
<p>"Just missing shots," Harden said. "Second half, I think I took two shots, but the ball was moving. We're getting better shots. So just picking and choosing my spots and figuring out what works, what doesn't work, for the betterment of the team."</p>
<p>Cavs coach Kenny Atkinson took the blame for Harden's lack of involvement in the offense in the second half.</p>
<p>"Can't have our Hall of Famer taking two shots in the second half," said Atkinson. "I'll take that one."</p>
<p>Game 3 takes place on Saturday.</p></description>

      <description><p>The Detroit Pistons defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers 107-97 to win Game 2 and take a 2-0 series lead in the Eastern Conference semifinals. </p>
<p>Cade Cunningham finished with 25 points and 10 assists, with Tobias Harris adding 21 points.</p>
<p>"We just wear on you," Pistons coach J.B. Bickerstaff said. "That's what our objective is."</p>
<p>The Pistons have now won five straight games since being down 3-1 against the Magic.</p>
<p>Donovan Mitchell scored 31 points for the Cavs, with Jarrett Allen adding 22 points and seven rebounds.</p>
<p>James Harden finished with just 10 points and missed 10-of-13 shots.</p>
<p>Cavs coach Kenny Atkinson said that the Cavs have to open games faster.</p>
<p>"I don't know what it is with the start of games," Atkinson said. "They came out super aggressive of course, but it's the playoffs. Obviously, we haven't figured that one out. Still back to the drawing board."</p>

   <item>
      <title>Tiago Splitter Unlikely To Receive Full-Time Head Coaching Job With Blazers</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285514/Tiago-Splitter-Unlikely-To-Receive-Full-Time-Head-Coaching-Job-With-Blazers</link>
      <description><p>Tiago Splitter, who guided the Portland Trail Blazers to a 42-39 record and the franchise's first playoff berth in five years, is unlikely to be offered the full-time head coaching position, sources with knowledge of the situation tell Jake Fischer. The Brazilian was elevated to interim head coach after felony charges were filed against Chauncey Billups following the season's opening game.</p>
<p>Despite remaining under consideration, sources continue to indicate Portland will look elsewhere for a permanent hire as the franchise pursues interviews with as many as 30 candidates.</p>
<p>Several NBA assistant coaches have already declined Portland's interview requests, citing new owner Tom Dundon's unconventional approach and persistent reports of below-market compensation for the role. Veteran coaches Tom Thibodeau and Michael Malone both rejected early outreach from Dundon, with sources saying both insisted they would not engage while Splitter remained as interim coach.</p>
<p>Brooklyn Nets assistant Steve Hetzel and Denver Nuggets assistant Jared Dudley are among the candidates drawing interest from Portland. Hetzel spent three seasons with the Blazers, two of which overlapped with Damian Lillard. Sources say Lillard, positioned to return to a prominent role with the organization, has submitted suggestions to the front office regarding the search.</p>
<p>Hetzel is also a candidate for the vacant New Orleans head coaching position, with Portland considered a fallback option if he does not land that role.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 07 May 2026 15:06:06 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285514/Tiago-Splitter-Unlikely-To-Receive-Full-Time-Head-Coaching-Job-With-Blazers</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Splitter_Tiago_por_251025.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Cavaliers Contacted Bucks About Giannis Antetokounmpo Before Trade Deadline</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285513/Cavaliers-Contacted-Bucks-About-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Before-Trade-Deadline</link>
      <description><p>The Cleveland Cavaliers reached out to the Milwaukee Bucks regarding a potential Giannis Antetokounmpo trade before the deadline, league sources say. Milwaukee's asking price included Evan Mobley and <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/tradeable">all of Cleveland's tradeable draft capital</a>, which include three picks and three swaps on draft night.</p>
<p>Multiple rival executives, when informed of that trade construction, said they would make the deal despite the steep cost.</p>
<p>The Cavaliers remain capable of advancing to the Eastern Conference finals without LeBron James for the first time since the 1991-92 season as they take on the Detroit Pistons in the second round after a challenging first round series against the Toronto Raptors.</p>
<p>A potential early exit, however, could place Cleveland in a situation similar to Milwaukee's current predicament. Donovan Mitchell has one guaranteed season remaining on his contract, followed by a $53.8 million player option for 2027-28, mirroring Antetokounmpo's contractual structure. Mitchell becomes extension-eligible on July 7, nearly four months before Antetokounmpo reaches that threshold.</p>
<p>Mitchell, 29, would be weighing that extension decision after a third consecutive spring without reaching the Eastern Conference finals if Cleveland is eliminated.</p>
<p>The Cavaliers' front office faces a defining offseason regardless of the playoff outcome. Whether pursuing Antetokounmpo or securing Mitchell's long-term commitment, Cleveland's front office faces critical roster decisions in the coming months.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 07 May 2026 14:59:41 EDT</pubDate>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Antetokounmpo_Giannis_mil_251210.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Charles Lee, Hornets Sign Contract Extension</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285512/Charles-Lee-Hornets-Sign-Contract-Extension</link>
      <description><p>Charles Lee and the Charlotte Hornets have signed a contract extension. While the Hornets didn't make it out of the play-in and into the playoffs, they were one of the best teams in the NBA after a slow start to the 2025-26 season.</p>
<p>The Hornets had a 25-win game improvement in Lee's second season as head coach. The Hornets hired Lee ahead of the 2024-25 season after he had assistant coach stints with the Atlanta Hawks, Milwaukee Bucks and Boston Celtics.</p>
<p>"Charles has done an outstanding job establishing a foundation for who we want to be as a team," said Jeff Peterson. "From day one, Charles and his staff have prioritized player development, creating an environment where each of our players are committed to getting better and continue to improve. He has built a team-first culture rooted in accountability, hard work and professionalism. I'm excited to keep working closely with Charles as we continue to build the Hornets for long-term success."</p>
<p>"I'm incredibly grateful for the opportunity to continue leading this team and building on what we've started here in Charlotte," said Lee. "I want to thank Rick, Gabe and Jeff for their support as well as my coaching staff and the players for their tireless work and dedication. I'm excited about the direction we're headed and our team's bright future. Our players have shown a real commitment to growth, and I'm proud of the culture we're establishing together. We're just getting started, and I'm looking forward to the work ahead."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 07 May 2026 14:46:57 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285512/Charles-Lee-Hornets-Sign-Contract-Extension</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Lee_Charles_mil_230227.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>OG Anunoby Day-To-Day With Hamstring Injury Ahead Of Game 3 At Sixers</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285511/OG-Anunoby-Day-To-Day-With-Hamstring-Injury-Ahead-Of-Game-3-At-Sixers</link>
      <description><p>New York Knicks forward OG Anunoby has been diagnosed with a right hamstring strain and will be monitored on a day-to-day basis, sources told ESPN's Shams Charania. Anunoby is listed as questionable for Game 3 on Friday in Philadelphia.</p>
<p>The strain is not considered serious, sources told Charania, allowing both the player and the team to assess his availability ahead of each game.</p>
<p>Anunoby departed Wednesday's 108-102 win in Game 2 after a dunk attempt left him visibly uncomfortable. He signaled to the bench for a substitution following a Mikal Bridges jumper that put New York up 103-99 with 2:56 remaining.</p>
<p>He finished with 24 points, five rebounds, four steals and a block across 37 minutes as the Knicks took a 2-0 series lead in the Eastern Conference semifinals.</p>
<p>"We'll see what it is, but it's next man up," Bridges said Wednesday night.</p>
<p>Anunoby entered as New York's second-leading playoff scorer behind Jalen Brunson, while also posting the team's best shooting efficiency at 64 percent from the field and nearly 60 percent from three-point range on roughly five attempts per game.</p>
<p>The injury carries historical weight for Anunoby. A left hamstring strain during the 2024 Eastern Conference semifinals against Indiana sidelined him for Games 3 through 6. He attempted a return in Game 7 at Madison Square Garden but lasted only five minutes before being removed as the Knicks lost.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 07 May 2026 14:40:26 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285511/OG-Anunoby-Day-To-Day-With-Hamstring-Injury-Ahead-Of-Game-3-At-Sixers</guid>

   </item>

   <item>
      <title>Spurs Win By 38 In Game 2 To Even Series, Chris Finch Tells Wolves They 'Got Punked'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285508/Spurs-Win-By-38-In-Game-2-To-Even-Series-Chris-Finch-Tells-Wolves-They-Got-Punked</link>
      <description><p>De'Aaron Fox and Victor Wembanyama rebounded from shaky Game 1 performances to power the San Antonio Spurs to a 133-95 blowout of the Minnesota Timberwolves on Wednesday night, evening the Western Conference semifinals at 1-1.</p>
<p>As the locker room cleared after Game 1's two-point loss, Wembanyama approached Fox at his locker to discuss the situation.</p>
<p>"We talked about how we were in a good place," Fox told ESPN. "We played like s---, and we lost by two."</p>
<p>After combining for just 21 points on 32.2% shooting in the opener, the All-Star duo combined to shoot 48% in Game 2. Fox finished with efficient scoring and early aggression, reeling off nine first-quarter points. Wembanyama posted 19 points, 15 rebounds and two blocks, his fourth consecutive postseason double-double with multiple blocks, a franchise streak not achieved since Tim Duncan in 2010.</p>
<p>Stephon Castle added a game-high 21 points, and the Spurs outscored Minnesota 34-8 in transition. San Antonio also tied its franchise playoff record with 16 three-pointers, led by Julian Champagnie's 4-of-6 performance.</p>
<p>"De'Aaron and Victor set a tone, and everyone followed suit," coach Mitch Johnson said.</p>
<p>The Timberwolves scored just 17 first-quarter points and 18 in the second, turned the ball over 22 times and trailed by 24 at halftime before the deficit ballooned to 47.</p>
<p>Wolves coach Chris Finch told his team afterward that they "got punked." Anthony Edwards had warned his teammates before tipoff.</p>
<p>"We came out cool," said Edwards. "Look what happened. My momma used to tell me that a hard head make a soft ass. That's what happened tonight."</p>
<p>Minnesota made just 9 of 30 three-point attempts, missed 26 of 44 shots in the paint and struggled against San Antonio's full-court pressure and selective double-teams on Edwards.</p>
<p>"Got to use it as a catalyst for ball movement," Finch said. "I thought we dribbled to tough spots. Our spacing around it wasn't really good."</p>
<p>Jaden McDaniels was one of Minnesota's brighter performers but was held to 20 minutes by foul trouble, a recurring issue. Edwards was direct about the cost.</p>
<p>"You need Jaden on the floor at all times," Edwards said. "When he gets in foul trouble, they get happy."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">Game 3 is Friday at Target Center with the Spurs favored by 3.5 points</a>.</p></description>

      <title>OG Anunoby Leaves Knicks' Game 2 Win With Leg Injury</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285505/OG-Anunoby-Leaves-Knicks-Game-2-Win-With-Leg-Injury</link>
      <description><p>OG Anunoby left the <a href="https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/48699866/knicks-og-anunoby-exits-game-2-late-apparent-leg-injury">New York Knicks' Game 2</a> win over the Philadelphia 76ers late in the fourth quarter due to an apparent leg injury.</p>
<p>Anunoby walked gingerly off the court after attempting a dunk with 2:56 left in the game.</p>
<p>The Knicks had no specific updates after the game.</p>
<p>"It looked like he was hopping, but I don't know anything," coach Mike Brown said. "They haven't told me. I just know he left the game. Nobody said he was back. I haven't talked to anybody yet."</p>
<p>Anunoby finished the game with 24 points and five rebounds.</p>
<p>Game 3 takes place on Friday. </p>
<p> </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 07 May 2026 01:11:43 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285505/OG-Anunoby-Leaves-Knicks-Game-2-Win-With-Leg-Injury</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Anuoby_OG_nyk_240102.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jalen Brunson Scores 26 Points In Knicks' Game 2 Win Over 76ers</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285504/Jalen-Brunson-Scores-26-Points-In-Knicks-Game-2-Win-Over-76ers</link>
      <description><p>The New York Knicks defeated the Philadelphia 76ers 108-102 in Game 2 to take a 2-0 series lead on Wednesday night.</p>
<p>The game featured 25 lead changes, the most for a playoff game in 11 years. Neither team lead by more than seven points in the game.</p>
<p>"Most importantly, it's staying poised, staying composed," Jalen Brunson, who scored 26 points, said. "Just figuring out one just play at a time, one step at a time and not looking too far ahead."</p>
<p>OG Anunoby had 24 points, though he left for the locker room late in the game due to an apparent leg injury. Coach Mike Brown had no status update afterwards.</p>
<p>76ers head coach Nick Nurse said that his team was getting good looks despite the loss.</p>
<p>"I thought we had maybe four wide-open shots in a row that didn't go," Nurse said. "We just needed to keep the scoreboard moving and we played great offense. We just didn't shotmake."</p>
<p>Joel Embiid missed the game with hip and ankle injuries.</p>
<p>Game 3 moves to Philadelphia on Friday with <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">the Sixers favored by 1.5 points</a> with the possibility of Anunoby being unavailable. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 07 May 2026 00:56:36 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285504/Jalen-Brunson-Scores-26-Points-In-Knicks-Game-2-Win-Over-76ers</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Brunson_Jalen_nyk_260501.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Bryson Graham Signals Full Rebuild Ahead For Bulls</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285503/Bryson-Graham-Signals-Full-Rebuild-Ahead-For-Bulls</link>
      <description><p>Chicago Bulls CEO Michael Reinsdorf publicly apologized to the franchise's fanbase Wednesday and accepted personal responsibility for the organization's prolonged struggles, speaking alongside Bryson Graham, the newly hired vice president of basketball operations, at an introductory news conference.</p>
<p>"It flows up to me, and I take responsibility," Reinsdorf said. "I do feel that today is a step in the right direction. It's an important step."</p>
<p>Graham, 39, wasted no time framing the challenge ahead.</p>
<p>"I'm not going to be up here and mince words and say we're further along if we just add a couple of pieces," Graham said. "Because that's not the case."</p>
<p>Graham spent time with the Atlanta Hawks and New Orleans Pelicans, rising from intern to general manager across both organizations. Reinsdorf described him as an elite talent evaluator who took no shortcuts throughout his career.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/chicago-bulls/4/playoff-history">The Bulls have not won a playoff series since 2014-15 and have reached the postseason just twice in the past decade</a>. Graham inherits a franchise with two 2026 first-round picks, including their own lottery selection and Portland's pick, plus roughly $60 million in projected cap space.</p>
<p>A head coach search begins next week and Graham signaled he would cast a broad net.</p>
<p>"Let's find the most competent and most talented people," Graham said. "That could be a coach you may not have even heard of, and I might get killed for it. But if I believe in him, I'm going to hire him."</p>
<p>Reinsdorf committed to funding a genuine championship contender, including a willingness to pay the luxury tax when warranted. Chicago has crossed that threshold only once in franchise history, in 2012-13.</p>
<p>"If we're competing for championships, we expect that we'll probably be in the luxury tax and totally OK with that," Reinsdorf said. "I don't want to be in luxury tax for a team that's not in the playoffs."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 06 May 2026 22:29:06 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285503/Bryson-Graham-Signals-Full-Rebuild-Ahead-For-Bulls</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_chicagobulls.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Bucks Co-Owner Jimmy Haslam Wants Giannis Antetokounmpo's Future Resolved Before NBA Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285502/Bucks-Co-Owner-Jimmy-Haslam-Wants-Giannis-Antetokounmpos-Future-Resolved-Before-NBA-Draft</link>
      <description><p>Milwaukee Bucks co-owner Jimmy Haslam said Wednesday the franchise hopes to resolve Giannis Antetokounmpo's future before next month's NBA draft, framing the decision as one that will shape the organization's <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Milwaukee-Bucks/16/roster-turnover">entire roster construction</a> approach this offseason. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/tradeable">The Bucks can trade up to three first round picks and three swaps on draft night</a> if they want to continue building around Antetokounmpo in the short- and medium-term.</p>
<p>"Sometime over the next six or seven weeks, we'll decide whether Giannis is going to sign a max contract and stay with us, or he's going to play somewhere else," Haslam said at a news conference introducing new head coach Taylor Jenkins. "Jon and Taylor, along with Wes and myself will make that call, and we understand the gravity of that call."</p>
<p>Haslam was direct about the stakes involved. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/283692/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Trade-At-Deadline-Could-Result-In-Other-Major-Deals-By-Contending-Teams">The Bucks received trade interest at the deadline in February from the Golden State Warriors, Miami Heat, New York Knicks and Minnesota Timberwolves</a>.</p>
<p>"If Giannis does play somewhere else, we've got to have a lot of assets," Haslam said. "That's Jon's job to do. And if he's here, then you build the team differently."</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo, 31, has repeatedly stated his desire to play for a championship-contending franchise. Milwaukee went 32-50 this season, snapping nine consecutive playoff appearances, while injuries held the two-time MVP to a career-low 36 games.</p>
<p>The Bucks can offer Antetokounmpo a four-year, $275 million extension in October. If unsigned, he would become a free agent after next season during the 2027 offseason.</p>
<p>Haslam emphasized the organization's relationship with the player remains open and direct.</p>
<p>"We never had any problem communicating directly with Giannis at all," Haslam said. "We've had those kind of conversations since the season was over. We will do what's best for Giannis and what's best for the organization."</p>
<p>Jenkins, who compiled a 250-214 record across six seasons with the Memphis Grizzlies, said Antetokounmpo has already reached out since the appointment.</p>
<p>"He's expressed tremendous excitement for me and my family," Jenkins said. "He and I established a really good relationship and maintained that respect, even from a distance."</p>
<p>Jenkins becomes Milwaukee's fourth head coach in five seasons, following Mike Budenholzer, Adrian Griffin and Doc Rivers.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 06 May 2026 21:59:33 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285502/Bucks-Co-Owner-Jimmy-Haslam-Wants-Giannis-Antetokounmpos-Future-Resolved-Before-NBA-Draft</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Antetokounmpo_Giannis_mil_250429.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jaylen Brown Says He Wants To Play In Boston For Next 10 Years, Explains 'Favorite Season' Comments</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285501/Jaylen-Brown-Says-He-Wants-To-Play-In-Boston-For-Next-10-Years-Explains-Favorite-Season-Comments</link>
      <description><p>Jaylen Brown directly addressed speculation about his future with the Boston Celtics on Wednesday night, declaring his desire to remain with the franchise long term and pushing back against reports of organizational tension.</p>
<p>"<a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285496/Brad-Stevens-Pushes-Back-On-Reports-Of-Jaylen-Brown-Rift-With-Celtics">I hate that our president of basketball operations even had to respond to this</a>," Brown said during a Twitch stream. "Me and Brad have a great relationship. I love Boston. And if it was up to me I could play in Boston for the next 10 years."</p>
<p>The comments came hours after Celtics president Brad Stevens said his Monday conversation with Brown was "nothing but positive," responding to Hall of Famer Tracy McGrady's claim on his "Cousins" podcast that Brown's frustration "lies deeply within the organization."</p>
<p>Brown also stood behind his earlier statement that this was his favorite season, a remark that drew scrutiny given Boston's stunning first-round exit against the Philadelphia 76ers and the Celtics' 2024 championship.</p>
<p>"You got to see all of these guys, all of my teammates, grow," Brown said. "I got to see them overcome adversity as a group, up close and personal. Obviously, we're not satisfied with the result. But to fight and maneuver through adversity and grow, and galvanize with a bunch of guys and to have that mindset and approach, this was my favorite season."</p>
<p>Brown also defended his postgame officiating criticism, for which the NBA fined him $50,000 Tuesday.</p>
<p>"The inconsistency of the officiating between the playoffs and regular season is not just something that's been talked about by me," Brown said. "You can fine me, you can continue to fine me, but I care about this s---. I love the game of basketball. Damn, fine me for that."</p>
<p>Brown posted career highs across the board this season, averaging 28.7 points, 6.9 rebounds and 5.1 assists while guiding Boston to a 56-26 record despite Jayson Tatum missing significant time recovering from a torn Achilles. He is eligible to sign a two-year, $141.9 million extension with the Celtics in July and <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/player-value-index-rankings/explained">ranks 13th in RealGM's NBA Player Value Index</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 06 May 2026 21:53:38 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285501/Jaylen-Brown-Says-He-Wants-To-Play-In-Boston-For-Next-10-Years-Explains-Favorite-Season-Comments</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Brown_Jaylen_bos_230321.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Christian Braun Takes Accountability For Poor Playoffs Amid Undisclosed Injury</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285500/Christian-Braun-Takes-Accountability-For-Poor-Playoffs-Amid-Undisclosed-Injury</link>
      <description><p>Christian Braun played through a severe left ankle sprain and an undisclosed left calf injury during the Denver Nuggets' first-round playoff loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves, contributing to a physically compromised and statistically underwhelming performance that has raised questions about his future with the franchise.</p>
<p>Denver fell to the Timberwolves 4-2, marking the franchise's first time they've lost a first round playoff series loss in four years. Braun averaged 8.3 points and 3.5 rebounds in 31 minutes per game, shooting poorly inside the arc and repeatedly pulling back from rim attacks that defined his best basketball.</p>
<p>Braun accepted full responsibility despite the physical limitations.</p>
<p>"It's just unacceptable. Especially with the talent we have on this roster," Braun said. "A first-round exit's not acceptable. We've gotta bounce back. We've gotta get to work. You can kind of put it on my shoulders."</p>
<p>Braun sustained the calf injury in Game 1 against Minnesota, compounding an ankle that had already cost him 38 regular season games. The left leg is his primary push-off foot for jumps. In the series' final four games he averaged just 5.5 points on 6-for-17 shooting.</p>
<p>"I could sit here and make every excuse," Braun said. "I could blame my ankle. I could blame injuries that people don't know about. But that doesn't really matter. If I'm going to be on the court, the expectation is to win."</p>
<p>Braun's five-year, $125 million rookie extension takes effect next season. Trading him would likely require attaching additional assets, a<a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/tradeable">s the Nuggets currently hold no future first-round picks eligible for trade</a> after using their 2032 selection to move Michael Porter Jr.'s contract last summer.</p>
<p>Braun's 2024-25 season remains his measuring stick. He led all NBA guards in true shooting percentage at 66.5% and topped the league in fast-break points. This season those numbers declined sharply, with his three-point percentage dropping from 39.7% to 30.1%.</p>
<p>"I was rewarded for my work on my rookie deal, so I understand the expectations are higher, and I need to be better," Braun said. "I get to get healthy, first and foremost. But there is no excuse."</p>
<p>The Nuggets face key roster decisions in the offseason, most notably the restricted free agency of Peyton Watson.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 06 May 2026 15:45:55 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285500/Christian-Braun-Takes-Accountability-For-Poor-Playoffs-Amid-Undisclosed-Injury</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Braun_Christian_den_251024.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jared McCain 'Definitely Hurt' By 'Sold High' Comment After Trade From Sixers</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285499/Jared-McCain-Definitely-Hurt-By-Sold-High-Comment-After-Trade-From-Sixers</link>
      <description><p>Jared McCain's arrival with the Oklahoma City reflects the kind of calculated, forward-looking roster construction that has defined Sam Presti's tenure. Philadelphia traded the young guard after VJ Edgecombe emerged as a backcourt cornerstone and McCain's injured knee raised durability concerns, with the Sixers receiving a 2026 Houston Rockets first-round pick and three second-rounders in return.</p>
<p>Sixers GM Daryl Morey declared publicly that Philadelphia had "sold high" on McCain. </p>
<p>"It definitely hurts," McCain said of the comment. "In my opinion, I try to prove the people right that really believe in me, whether it's my family, my support system. It's not about proving the team wrong or whatever, proving the GM wrong. Whatever he said, that's his opinion. I just try and be myself and not worry about that."</p>
<p>McCain had finished seventh in Rookie of the Year voting the previous season despite appearing in just 23 games, a performance that kept him on the radar of teams willing to bet on his potential.</p>
<p>McCain connected on nearly 40 percent of his three-point attempts since joining the Thunder, providing reliable perimeter shooting during the team's regular season stretch run. </p>
<p>McCain has embraced the environment around a veteran roster with championship experience.</p>
<p>"It's been amazing, man," McCain said. "It's been a blessing just to get to learn from these guys. They already have championships, and just being able to soak in whatever they're able to tell me and ask questions and do as much as I can to contribute."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 06 May 2026 15:29:27 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285499/Jared-McCain-Definitely-Hurt-By-Sold-High-Comment-After-Trade-From-Sixers</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/McCain_Jared_okc_260225.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Luka Doncic Reveals PRP Treatment In Spain, Remains Day-To-Day With Grade 2 Hamstring Strain</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285498/Luka-Doncic-Reveals-PRP-Treatment-In-Spain-Remains-Day-To-Day-With-Grade-2-Hamstring-Strain</link>
      <description><p>Los Angeles Lakers star Luka Doncic offered his first public comments since suffering a Grade 2 hamstring strain on April 2, telling reporters Wednesday he has no firm return date and remains frustrated watching his teammates compete in the playoffs without him.</p>
<p>Doncic traveled to Spain following the injury to receive platelet-rich plasma therapy, undergoing four PRP injections spaced four days apart before rejoining the team.</p>
<p>"I went to Spain to do PRP," Doncic said. "Everybody knows that Spain, they're just one of the best countries to do that. And obviously, I talked with the Lakers' doctors, so everybody agreed for me to go there."</p>
<p>The initial prognosis called for an eight-week recovery. Game 2 against Oklahoma City on Thursday marks five weeks since the injury occurred. Doncic said he has begun running but has not participated in any contact drills, and was noncommittal when asked about a potential return for Games 3 and 4 in Los Angeles.</p>
<p>"It's a tough one for me because I came back from injuries before too soon, and it wasn't the best result," Doncic said. "You have to be very careful. All the recovery, the chamber, cold tub, everything I can to come back, but it's obviously very different than other injuries I had."</p>
<p>The Lakers dropped Game 1 to Oklahoma City 108-90, and LeBron James acknowledged the gap left by a player who averaged 34 points, 9 assists and 8 rebounds this season.</p>
<p>"When you play against the world champions and miss having a guy that averages 34 and eight and nine and is that special, that's a major piece missing," James said.</p>
<p>Doncic remained proud of his teammates despite his own distress.</p>
<p>"It's very frustrating seeing what my team is doing," Doncic said. "I'm very proud of them. It's been very tough to just sit and watch them play."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 06 May 2026 14:19:51 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285498/Luka-Doncic-Reveals-PRP-Treatment-In-Spain-Remains-Day-To-Day-With-Grade-2-Hamstring-Strain</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Doncic_Luka_lal_260225.jpg" />
   </item>
